label: European Research Council (ERC) Frontier Grants
version: ERC Work Programme 2026 (B1/B2 templates; calls opening July 2025 onward)

sections.title: Cover page (B1)
sections.purpose: Title, synopsis, panel and keywords used for assignment; not counted in the page limit.
mustContain: Proposal title
Short synopsis / abstract
Chosen evaluation panel
Keywords

sections.title: Part B1 — Part I: Extended Synopsis (Step 1)
sections.purpose: Self-contained case for the idea — WHAT is this about — judged at Step 1.
mustContain: State of the art and the precise unsolved gap (why it has resisted solution, why now, why you)
Central objective/hypothesis broken into 3–4 falsifiable, measurable objectives
Overall strategy at a high level
Explicit argument for why the project is ground-breaking and ambitious
What becomes possible / what understanding changes if it succeeds
Breakthrough stated in the opening paragraph, readable by a non-specialist

sections.title: Part B1 — Curriculum Vitae + Track Record (Step 1)
sections.purpose: WHO are you — evidence of capacity to deliver frontier research at this career stage.
mustContain: Narrative arguing 'I am the person who can do THIS project'
Best/most significant outputs (not a publication count), with the PI's role
Independence and leadership appropriate to the scheme (StG/CoG/AdG)
Diverse research outputs: prizes, invited talks, supervision, funding won, datasets/software/IP
Career breaks and field-specific norms flagged

sections.title: Part B2 — Part II: Scientific Proposal / Methodology (Step 2)
sections.purpose: HOW will you do it — detailed methodology and feasibility, evaluated only if B1 passes to Step 2.
mustContain: Detailed methodology with objective→method→expected-outcome logic; each method tied to a specific objective
Work plan / work packages and team roles
Preliminary / pilot data supporting feasibility
Risks and contingencies: likelihood, impact and a concrete results-preserving fallback per major risk
Timeline / Gantt over 60 months with milestones, deliverables and go/no-go decision gates
Infrastructure and host environment

sections.title: Funding ID / appendices
sections.purpose: Supporting administrative information; no page limit.
mustContain: Funding ID and supporting appendices
No hidden scientific content (panels are instructed to ignore science smuggled here)

sections.title: Budget / resources justification
sections.purpose: Justify the requested resources; sits outside the page count.
mustContain: Resources matched to and justified by the work plan
Personnel, equipment and infrastructure aligned with the scheme budget ceiling

sections.title: Ethics & security
sections.purpose: Declare and address ethics and security issues raised by the project.
mustContain: Ethics self-assessment / issues table
Security-sensitive aspects addressed where applicable

reviewCriteria: Research project: ground-breaking nature, ambition and feasibility (feasibility weighed at Step 2)
Principal Investigator: intellectual capacity, creativity and commitment
Excellence is the sole criterion — there is no separate impact or implementation pillar
Step 1 marks: A (passes to Step 2) / B (weaknesses, not retained) / C (significant weaknesses, rejected)
Step 2 marks: A (fundable if budget allows) / B (weaknesses preventing funding); the scientific proposal is scored while CV/Track Record receives qualitative feedback only
Panels rank on overall strengths/weaknesses, not arithmetic — weaknesses predict the outcome more than strengths; one fatal flaw sinks the proposal

checklist.check: Part B1 Part I (Extended Synopsis) is at most 5 pages.
Part B1 CV + Track Record is at most ~4 pages.
Part B2 Part II is at most 7 pages (10 for Synergy).
All required sections are present: Extended Synopsis, CV/Track Record, Methodology, Risk & Contingency, and Timeline/Gantt.
Every citation resolves to a real reference (DOI/PMID exists).
Uses the current-year official B1/B2 templates without rebuilding them.
Correct scheme and eligibility window for the PI (incl. parental-leave / illness / victims-of-violence extensions where applicable).
No scientific content is hidden in the Funding ID or appendices to dodge the page limits.
The proposal explicitly states what becomes possible / how the field changes if it succeeds — ground-breaking nature is substantiated, not merely asserted.
The risk is conceptual (success genuinely uncertain even with unlimited resources), not operational; it is not a hypothesis-free 'fishing expedition'.
One crisp overarching goal/hypothesis broken into 3–4 coherent, measurable, falsifiable objectives — not a laundry list of loosely-linked aims.
State of the art defines the gap sharply, is current and global, and engages competing groups / likely reviewers fairly without overclaiming 'first ever'.
In B2 every method is tied to a specific objective with objective→method→expected-outcome logic, and technique choices are justified.
B2 includes preliminary / pilot data supporting feasibility.
Each major risk states likelihood, impact and a concrete results-preserving fallback; the proposal does not claim 'no significant risks'.
A realistic 60-month Gantt maps work packages, milestones, deliverables, dependencies and go/no-go decision gates (incl. hiring/lab-setup time).
The CV is a narrative arguing independence and leadership for the career stage and capacity for THIS project — not an undifferentiated publication dump.
Readable by the multidisciplinary panel majority: breakthrough front-loaded, acronyms defined, no jargon walls.
Not a recycled national or Horizon-collaborative proposal — the conceptual frame is ERC-specific.

redFlags: Interesting but incremental — could appear in a normal national grant rather than change the field
No testable hypothesis / 'we will collect data and see' → fishing expedition
Ambition claimed but never substantiated; no 'if successful, the field changes because…'
Methods imply the answer is already known → no real conceptual risk
'No significant risks foreseen' → reads as not ambitious enough
Overclaiming novelty a referee can disprove ('first ever…')
Objectives that don't map to methods that don't map to the timeline
CV is a publication dump; independence/leadership not evident for the career stage
Scientific content hidden in appendices / Funding ID to dodge page limits
Reads like a recycled Horizon or national proposal
Specialist-only prose the panel majority cannot follow
requiredAttachments: Part B1 — Curriculum Vitae + Track Record (≤~4 pp)
Part B2 — Scientific Proposal / methodology (≤7 pp; 10 for Synergy)
Funding ID / appendices (no hidden scientific content)
Budget / resources justification (outside the page count)
recencyNote: Encodes ERC Work Programme 2026; only the current-year Work Programme, the Information for Applicants for the specific call, and the live B1/B2 templates are binding. Re-verify each cycle: budgets and page limits; that feasibility is assessed only at Step 2; the new AdG Step-2 interview; CV/Track Record giving qualitative feedback (not a score); eligibility windows and extensions (2027 plans: StG 0–10 yrs and CoG 5–15 yrs post-PhD, max one StG and one CoG per career); consolidated additional funding (up to €1M, or up to €2M for relocation); and outcome-dependent resubmission lock-outs.
